LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A., AN ACQUIRER OF CERTAIN ASSESTS AND LIABILITIES OF WASHINGTON MUTUAL BANK AND CALIFORNIA RECONVEYANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 1 BECAUSE DEFENDANT'S OWE NO DUTY IN NEGLIGENCE. SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 2, AS TO JPMC IF PLAINTIFF CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH THAT PLAINTIFF WAS TOLD THAT IF SHE DEFAULTED, JPMC WOULD GIVE HER DILLIGENT AND GOOD FAITH CONSIDERATION AND AS TO CRC TO STATE SOME BASIS FOR ITS LIABILITY. SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 3 AND 4 IN LIGHT OF CAUSE OF ACTION 2. MOVING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189 (301/PJB)

MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Arthur Nathaniel Bortz, Esq. of Law Offices of Arnold Laub, 415-362-0101 ext. 307 for plaintiff and moving party Sepideh Valentino

RULING:
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Why Preliminary Injunction Should Not Issue IS GRANTED. PROOF OF SERVICE CONFORMING TO REQUIREMENTS OF THE 6/29 EX PARTE ORDER WAS PRESENTED AT THE HEARING AS REQUIRED BY THE TENTATIVE RULING. BOND TO BE SET AT $5,000. INJUNCTION WOULD CONTINUE DURING THE PENDENCY OF THIS MATTER ABSENT FURTHER ORDER OF THIS COURT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189(301/PJB)
